Swift 6Kotlin 2.xPython 3.13 
import Foundation

// Current date
let now = Date()
let nowInstant = Date.now                      // Swift 5.7+

// Date components
let calendar = Calendar.current
let components = calendar.dateComponents([.year, .month, .day, .hour], from: now)
let year  = components.year!
let month = components.month!

// Creating a date from components
var dc = DateComponents()
dc.year = 2026; dc.month = 3; dc.day = 15; dc.hour = 10
dc.timeZone = TimeZone(identifier: "Europe/Berlin")
let specificDate = calendar.date(from: dc)!

// Formatting (FormatStyle Swift 5.5+)
let formatted = now.formatted(
    .dateTime.day().month(.wide).year()
)
// "March 19, 2026"

let custom = now.formatted(
    Date.FormatStyle()
        .year(.defaultDigits)
        .month(.twoDigits)
        .day(.twoDigits)
        .hour(.twoDigits(amPM: .omitted))
        .minute(.twoDigits)
)

// ISO 8601
let iso = now.ISO8601Format()

// Parsing
let parsed = try Date("2026-03-15T10:30:00Z", strategy: .iso8601)

// Duration / arithmetic
let tomorrow = calendar.date(byAdding: .day, value: 1, to: now)!
let nextWeek = calendar.date(byAdding: .weekOfYear, value: 1, to: now)!
let diff = calendar.dateComponents([.day, .hour], from: now, to: tomorrow)

// Clock API (Swift 5.7+)
let clock = ContinuousClock()
let elapsed = try await clock.measure {
    try await Task.sleep(for: .seconds(1))
}
print("Elapsed: \(elapsed)")                   // "Elapsed: 1.0 seconds"

import java.time.*
import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit

// Current date/time
val now      = LocalDateTime.now()
val today    = LocalDate.now()
val time     = LocalTime.now()
val instant  = Instant.now()                   // UTC timestamp

// With timezone
val berlinNow = ZonedDateTime.now(ZoneId.of("Europe/Berlin"))

// Creation
val specific = LocalDateTime.of(2026, Month.MARCH, 15, 10, 30)
val date     = LocalDate.of(2026, 3, 15)

// Components
val year  = now.year
val month = now.month                          // Month enum
val day   = now.dayOfMonth
val dow   = now.dayOfWeek                      // DayOfWeek enum

// Formatting
val formatted = now.format(DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd.MM.yyyy HH:mm"))
val iso       = now.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME)

// Parsing
val parsed = LocalDate.parse("2026-03-15")
val parsed2 = LocalDateTime.parse("15.03.2026 10:30",
                DateTimeFormatter.ofPattern("dd.MM.yyyy HH:mm"))

// Arithmetic
val tomorrow = today.plusDays(1)
val nextWeek = today.plusWeeks(1)
val ago      = today.minusMonths(3)

// Duration (for time) and Period (for date)
val duration = Duration.between(now, now.plusHours(5))
val period   = Period.between(date, today)

val daysBetween = ChronoUnit.DAYS.between(date, today)

// kotlinx-datetime (multiplatform)
import kotlinx.datetime.*

val nowKx = Clock.System.now()                 // Instant
val tz    = TimeZone.of("Europe/Berlin")
val local = nowKx.toLocalDateTime(tz)

from datetime import datetime, date, time, timedelta, timezone
from zoneinfo import ZoneInfo                  # Python 3.9+ (built in)
from dateutil.relativedelta import relativedelta  # pip install python-dateutil

# Current date/time
now   = datetime.now()
today = date.today()
utc   = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

# With timezone
berlin = datetime.now(ZoneInfo("Europe/Berlin"))

# Creation
specific = datetime(2026, 3, 15, 10, 30, 0, tzinfo=ZoneInfo("Europe/Berlin"))

# Components
year  = now.year
month = now.month
day   = now.day
dow   = now.weekday()                          # 0=Mon, 6=Sun
dow2  = now.isoweekday()                       # 1=Mon, 7=Sun

# Formatting
formatted = now.strftime("%d.%m.%Y %H:%M")    # "19.03.2026 21:34"
iso       = now.isoformat()                    # "2026-03-19T21:34:59"

# Parsing
parsed = datetime.strptime("15.03.2026 10:30", "%d.%m.%Y %H:%M")
parsed2 = date.fromisoformat("2026-03-15")
parsed3 = datetime.fromisoformat("2026-03-15T10:30:00+01:00")  # 3.11+

# Arithmetic
tomorrow  = today + timedelta(days=1)
next_week = today + timedelta(weeks=1)
diff      = datetime(2026, 12, 31) - now       # timedelta

# relativedelta — months and years (timedelta cannot do that)
next_month = today + relativedelta(months=1)
next_year  = today + relativedelta(years=1)
age = relativedelta(today, date(1990, 1, 1))
print(f"{age.years} years, {age.months} months")

# Timestamp (Unix epoch)
ts = now.timestamp()                           # float
from_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts, tz=timezone.utc)

# Performance timing
import time
start = time.perf_counter()
heavy_work()
elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
print(f"{elapsed:.4f}s")

# monotonic (independent of the system clock)
mono_start = time.monotonic()

// QR code generation via Core Image
func generateQR(from string: String, size: CGSize = CGSize(width: 200, height: 200)) -> UIImage? {
    guard let data = string.data(using: .ascii),
          let filter = CIFilter(name: "CIQRCodeGenerator")
    else { return nil }
    filter.setValue(data, forKey: "inputMessage")
    filter.setValue("H", forKey: "inputCorrectionLevel")
    guard let output = filter.outputImage else { return nil }

    let scaleX = size.width  / output.extent.size.width
    let scaleY = size.height / output.extent.size.height
    let scaled = output.transformed(by: CGAffineTransform(scaleX: scaleX, y: scaleY))
    guard let cgOut = CIContext().createCGImage(scaled, from: scaled.extent)
    else { return nil }
    return UIImage(cgImage: cgOut)
}

// Saving
fun Bitmap.saveAsJpeg(path: String, quality: Int = 85) {
    java.io.FileOutputStream(path).use { fos ->
        compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.JPEG, quality, fos)
    }
}

fun Bitmap.saveAsPng(path: String) {
    java.io.FileOutputStream(path).use { fos ->
        compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.PNG, 100, fos)
    }
}

fun Bitmap.toBase64Jpeg(quality: Int = 80): String {
    val stream = java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream()
    compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.JPEG, quality, stream)
    return android.util.Base64.encodeToString(
        stream.toByteArray(), android.util.Base64.DEFAULT
    )
}

)

# QR with a centered logo
def qr_with_logo(data: str, logo_path: str, output: str) -> None:
    qr = qrcode.QRCode(error_correction=qrcode.constants.ERROR_CORRECT_H)
    qr.add_data(data)
    qr.make(fit=True)
    qr_img = qr.make_image(fill_color="black", back_color="white").convert("RGBA")

    logo = Image.open(logo_path).convert("RGBA")
    logo_size = qr_img.size[0] // 4
    logo = logo.resize((logo_size, logo_size), Image.LANCZOS)

    pos = ((qr_img.size[0] - logo_size) // 2, (qr_img.size[1] - logo_size) // 2)
    qr_img.paste(logo, pos, mask=logo)
    qr_img.save(output)

Kotlin Only

KMP is a unique Kotlin capability for cross-platform development: one codebase for iOS, Android, Desktop, and Web.

// ── Project structure ──
// my-project/
// ├── shared/                    ← shared KMP module
// │   ├── src/
// │   │   ├── commonMain/        ← shared code (all platforms)
// │   │   ├── commonTest/
// │   │   ├── androidMain/       ← Android-specific
// │   │   ├── iosMain/           ← iOS-specific
// │   │   └── desktopMain/       ← Desktop-specific
// ├── androidApp/                ← Android application
// ├── iosApp/                    ← iOS application (Xcode)
// └── desktopApp/                ← Desktop application

// shared/build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    androidTarget { compilations.all { kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "17" } } }
    iosX64(); iosArm64(); iosSimulatorArm64()
    jvm("desktop")

    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-core:1.9.0")
            implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-serialization-json:1.7.3")
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-core:3.0.0")
        }
        androidMain.dependencies {
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-android:3.0.0")
        }
        iosMain.dependencies {
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-darwin:3.0.0")
        }
    }
}

// ── expect / actual — platform-dependent code ──

// commonMain: declare the “contract”
expect class PlatformInfo() {
    val name: String
    val version: String
}

expect fun currentTimeMillis(): Long

// androidMain: Android implementation
actual class PlatformInfo actual constructor() {
    actual val name = "Android"
    actual val version = android.os.Build.VERSION.RELEASE
}

actual fun currentTimeMillis(): Long = System.currentTimeMillis()

// iosMain: iOS implementation
actual class PlatformInfo actual constructor() {
    actual val name = "iOS"
    actual val version = UIDevice.currentDevice.systemVersion
}

actual fun currentTimeMillis(): Long =
    (NSDate().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000).toLong()

// ── Shared code (commonMain) ──

interface UserRepository {
    suspend fun getUsers(): List<User>
    suspend fun getUserById(id: Int): User?
}

class UserViewModel(private val repo: UserRepository) {
    private val _state = MutableStateFlow<UserState>(UserState.Loading)
    val state: StateFlow<UserState> = _state.asStateFlow()

    fun loadUsers() {
        viewModelScope.launch {
            _state.value = try {
                UserState.Success(repo.getUsers())
            } catch (e: Exception) {
                UserState.Error(e.message ?: "Unknown error")
            }
        }
    }
}

sealed class UserState {
    data object Loading : UserState()
    data class Success(val users: List<User>) : UserState()
    data class Error(val message: String) : UserState()
}

AES-GCM

Authenticated encryption (AEAD): encrypts data and verifies its integrity at the same time. Recommended for all new projects.

Swift 6Kotlin 2.xPython 3.13Go 1.24+ 
import CryptoKit

func encrypt(_ plaintext: Data, with key: SymmetricKey) throws -> Data {
    let sealedBox = try AES.GCM.seal(plaintext, using: key)
    return sealedBox.combined!                 // nonce + ciphertext + tag
}

func decrypt(_ cipherData: Data, with key: SymmetricKey) throws -> Data {
    let sealedBox = try AES.GCM.SealedBox(combined: cipherData)
    return try AES.GCM.open(sealedBox, using: key)
}

let symmetricKey = SymmetricKey(size: .bits256)
let secret = "Secret message".data(using: .utf8)!
let encrypted = try encrypt(secret, with: symmetricKey)
let base64     = encrypted.base64EncodedString()

let decrypted = try decrypt(Data(base64Encoded: base64)!, with: symmetricKey)
let original  = String(data: decrypted, encoding: .utf8)  // "Secret message"

// ChaCha20-Poly1305 — faster on mobile devices without AES hardware acceleration
let chaBox = try ChaChaPoly.seal(secret, using: symmetricKey)
let decoded = try ChaChaPoly.open(chaBox, using: symmetricKey)

import javax.crypto.*
import javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec

object AesGcm {
    private const val ALGORITHM = "AES/GCM/NoPadding"
    private const val KEY_SIZE  = 256
    private const val NONCE_LEN = 12           // 96 bit — recommended
    private const val TAG_LEN   = 128          // bits

    fun generateKey(): SecretKey =
        KeyGenerator.getInstance("AES").apply { init(KEY_SIZE) }.generateKey()

    fun encrypt(plaintext: ByteArray, key: SecretKey): ByteArray {
        val nonce  = ByteArray(NONCE_LEN).also {
            java.security.SecureRandom().nextBytes(it)
        }
        val cipher = Cipher.getInstance(ALGORITHM).apply {
            init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key, GCMParameterSpec(TAG_LEN, nonce))
        }
        val ciphertext = cipher.doFinal(plaintext)
        return nonce + ciphertext              // nonce || ciphertext+tag
    }

    fun decrypt(combined: ByteArray, key: SecretKey): ByteArray {
        val nonce      = combined.copyOfRange(0, NONCE_LEN)
        val ciphertext = combined.copyOfRange(NONCE_LEN, combined.size)
        return Cipher.getInstance(ALGORITHM).run {
            init(Cipher.DECRYPT_MODE, key, GCMParameterSpec(TAG_LEN, nonce))
            doFinal(ciphertext)
        }
    }
}

val key       = AesGcm.generateKey()
val encrypted = AesGcm.encrypt("Secret message".toByteArray(), key)
val decrypted = AesGcm.decrypt(encrypted, key)
println(String(decrypted))                     // "Secret message"

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
import secrets, base64

def encrypt(plaintext: bytes, key: bytes | None = None) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Returns (key, nonce+ciphertext)"""
    if key is None:
        key = AESGCM.generate_key(bit_length=256)
    aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
    nonce  = secrets.token_bytes(12)           # 96 bit
    ct     = aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, associated_data=None)
    return key, nonce + ct

def decrypt(combined: bytes, key: bytes) -> bytes:
    nonce, ct = combined[:12], combined[12:]
    return AESGCM(key).decrypt(nonce, ct, associated_data=None)

key, enc = encrypt(b"Secret message")
b64_enc  = base64.b64encode(enc).decode()
dec = decrypt(base64.b64decode(b64_enc), key)
print(dec.decode())                            // "Secret message"

// ③ AES-GCM — symmetric encryption
// GCM = Galois/Counter Mode: authenticated encryption (encryption + MAC)
// IMPORTANT: never reuse a nonce with the same key!
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func encrypt(plaintext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
    if len(key) != 16 && len(key) != 24 && len(key) != 32 {
        return nil, errors.New("key must be 16, 24, or 32 bytes (AES-128/192/256)")
    }

    block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    // Nonce: 12 bytes, cryptographically random
    nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())    // NonceSize() = 12
    if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("generate nonce: %w", err)
    }

    // Seal: encrypts AND appends the authentication tag (16 bytes at the end)
    // Format: [nonce(12) || ciphertext || tag(16)]
    ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nonce, nonce, plaintext, nil)
    return ciphertext, nil
}

func decrypt(ciphertext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
    block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    nonceSize := gcm.NonceSize()
    if len(ciphertext) < nonceSize {
        return nil, errors.New("ciphertext too short")
    }

    nonce, ciphertext := ciphertext[:nonceSize], ciphertext[nonceSize:]

    // Open: decrypts AND verifies the authentication tag
    plaintext, err := gcm.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, nil)
    if err != nil {
        // Do not leak details — only "decryption failed"
        return nil, errors.New("decryption failed")
    }
    return plaintext, nil
}

// Key derived from a password via PBKDF2/Argon2
func deriveKey(password, salt []byte) []byte {
    // Argon2id → 32-byte key for AES-256
    return argon2.IDKey(password, salt, 3, 64*1024, 4, 32)
}

func encryptWithPassword(plaintext []byte, password string) ([]byte, error) {
    salt := make([]byte, 16)
    if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, salt); err != nil { return nil, err }

    key := deriveKey([]byte(password), salt)
    encrypted, err := encrypt(plaintext, key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    // Format: [salt(16) || encrypted_data]
    result := make([]byte, len(salt)+len(encrypted))
    copy(result, salt)
    copy(result[len(salt):], encrypted)
    return result, nil
}

func decryptWithPassword(data []byte, password string) ([]byte, error) {
    if len(data) < 16 { return nil, errors.New("data too short") }
    salt, encrypted := data[:16], data[16:]
    key := deriveKey([]byte(password), salt)
    return decrypt(encrypted, key)
}

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ④ JWT — github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5
// ES256 (asymmetric) is recommended for production
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
type Claims struct {
    UserID string `json:"uid"`
    Email  string `json:"email"`
    Role   string `json:"role"`
    jwt.RegisteredClaims
}

type JWTManager struct {
    signingKey     []byte         // for HS256
    accessExpiry   time.Duration
    refreshExpiry  time.Duration
    issuer         string
}

func NewJWTManager(secret string) *JWTManager {
    return &JWTManager{
        signingKey:    []byte(secret),
        accessExpiry:  15 * time.Minute,  // short lifetime!
        refreshExpiry: 7 * 24 * time.Hour,
        issuer:        "myapp",
    }
}

func (m *JWTManager) GenerateAccessToken(userID, email, role string) (string, error) {
    claims := Claims{
        UserID: userID,
        Email:  email,
        Role:   role,
        RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
            Issuer:    m.issuer,
            Subject:   userID,
            ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(m.accessExpiry)),
            IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
            NotBefore: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
            ID:        generateTokenID(),           // jti: unique ID for revocation
        },
    }
    token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
    return token.SignedString(m.signingKey)
}

func (m *JWTManager) Verify(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
    token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(
        tokenString,
        &Claims{},
        func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
            // MUST verify the algorithm — protection against alg=none attacks
            if _, ok := t.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
                return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected signing method: %v", t.Header["alg"])
            }
            return m.signingKey, nil
        },
        jwt.WithIssuer(m.issuer),
        jwt.WithExpirationRequired(),          // require exp claim
        jwt.WithValidMethods([]string{"HS256"}), // algorithm whitelist
    )

    if err != nil {
        switch {
        case errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrTokenExpired):
            return nil, ErrTokenExpired
        case errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrTokenSignatureInvalid):
            return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
        default:
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse token: %w", err)
        }
    }

    claims, ok := token.Claims.(*Claims)
    if !ok || !token.Valid {
        return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
    }
    return claims, nil
}

// JWT Middleware for net/http
func (m *JWTManager) Middleware(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
        if authHeader == "" || !strings.HasPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ") {
            http.Error(w, `{"error":"missing authorization header"}`,
                http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }

        tokenString := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
        claims, err := m.Verify(tokenString)
        if err != nil {
            http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(`{"error":%q}`, err.Error()),
                http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }

        // Add claims to context
        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), claimsKey, claims)
        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    })
}

// Sentinel errors for JWT
var (
    ErrTokenExpired = errors.New("token expired")
    ErrTokenInvalid = errors.New("token invalid")
)

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ⑤ Secure random data generation
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func secureRandom() {
    // Session token
    token := make([]byte, 32)
    io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, token)
    sessionToken := base64.URLEncoding.EncodeToString(token) // URL-safe

    // Random string (for CSRF tokens, API keys)
    apiKey := make([]byte, 24)
    io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, apiKey)
    key := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(apiKey)

    // Random number in the range [0, max)
    import "math/big"
    max := big.NewInt(1_000_000)
    n, _ := rand.Int(rand.Reader, max)        // cryptographically secure!
    otp := n.Int64()                          // OTP code

    _ = sessionToken; _ = key; _ = otp
}

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ⑥ constant-time comparison — against timing attacks
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func safeCompare(a, b string) bool {
    // subtle.ConstantTimeCompare — same time regardless of equality
    return subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(a), []byte(b)) == 1
}

func validateAPIKey(provided, expected string) bool {
    // NEVER use == to compare secrets!
    // if provided == expected { ... }        // VULNERABLE to timing attacks
    return safeCompare(provided, expected)    // SAFE
}

Swift Only

Macros are a unique Swift capability. Kotlin uses KSP/KotlinPoet for code generation, and Python uses decorators and metaclasses.

// ── Built-in / Apple macros ──

// @Observable (Swift 5.9+, replaces ObservableObject + @Published)
import Observation

@Observable
class ViewModel {
    var items: [Item] = []
    var isLoading: Bool = false
    var errorMessage: String? = nil

    func load() async {
        isLoading = true
        defer { isLoading = false }
        items = try! await fetchItems()
    }
}

// In SwiftUI — @StateObject / @ObservedObject are not needed
struct ContentView: View {
    @State private var vm = ViewModel()

    var body: some View {
        if vm.isLoading { ProgressView() }
        else { List(vm.items) { Text($0.name) } }
    }
}

// #Preview (Swift 5.9+)
#Preview {
    ContentView()
}

// #stringify — built-in expression macro
let (value, string) = #stringify(2 + 3)
// value = 5, string = "2 + 3"

// ── Creating a custom macro ──
// Requires a separate Package target of type .macro

import SwiftSyntax
import SwiftSyntaxMacros

public struct UnwrappedMacro: ExpressionMacro {
    public static func expansion(
        of node: some FreestandingMacroExpansionSyntax,
        in context: some MacroExpansionContext
    ) throws -> ExprSyntax {
        let arg = node.argumentList.first!.expression
        return "\(arg) ?? { fatalError(\"Unexpected nil: \(arg)\") }()"
    }
}

// Macro declaration in the public API
@freestanding(expression)
public macro Unwrapped<T>(_ value: T?) -> T = #externalMacro(
    module: "MyMacrosImpl",
    type: "UnwrappedMacro"
)

// Usage
let value = #Unwrapped(optionalValue)

// ── Attached macros ──

@attached(extension, conformances: Equatable, names: named(==))
public macro AutoEquatable() = #externalMacro(
    module: "MyMacrosImpl", type: "AutoEquatableMacro"
)

@AutoEquatable
struct Point {
    var x: Double
    var y: Double
    var label: String
    // Generated code:
    // static func == (lhs: Point, rhs: Point) -> Bool {
    //     lhs.x == rhs.x && lhs.y == rhs.y && lhs.label == rhs.label
    // }
}

// Macro types:
// @freestanding(expression) — #macro() in expressions
// @freestanding(declaration) — #macro() for declarations
// @attached(member)          — adds members to a type
// @attached(memberAttribute) — adds attributes to members
// @attached(accessor)        — adds get/set
// @attached(extension)       — adds an extension with conformance
// @attached(peer)            — generates new declarations next to it

docs.text:
Synchronisierte Tabs

Unsere Codeblöcke sind in Tabs gruppiert: Swift 6, Kotlin 2.x, Python 3.13 und Go 1.24+. Laufzeit (None) Kopierverhalten Value Types kopieren bei Zuweisung (COW-Optimierung) Objekte werden als Referenz übergeben Alle Objekte per Referenz; Zahlen/Strings — unveränderlich

CPython Integer Caching

CPython speichert int Objekte im Bereich von −5 … 256 zwischen und internt kurze Bezeichner. Das bedeutet, a = 256; b = 256; a is b gibt True zurück (selbes Objekt), während a = 257; b = 257; a is b False liefert (zwei verschiedene Objekte). Verwenden Sie immer == statt is, um Werte zu vergleichen.

let a: Int = 10
let b: Int = 3

// Arithmetik

let div  = a / b          // 3  — Ganzzahldivision!

// Bit-Operationen
let and = a & b            // 2
let or  = a | b            // 11

// Mathematik

let root    = sqrt(Double(a))            // 3.162...

let rounded = Double(a).rounded()        // 10.0

// Überlaufsichere Operatoren (Wrap-around statt Absturz)

// Bereichsbeschränkung

val b: Int = 3

import kotlin.math.*
val power   = a.toDouble().pow(b.toDouble())  // 1000.0
val root    = sqrt(a.toDouble())              // 3.162...
val absVal  = abs(-5)                         // 5
val rounded = round(3.7)                      // 4.0

// Nützliche Extension-Funktionen

// Infinity und NaN (wie in Java)
val inf = Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY
val nan = Double.NaN
nan.isNaN()                             // true

a: int = 10
b: int = 3

s   = a + b            # 13
d   = a // b           # 3  — Abgerundete Division (immer abgerundet!)
fd  = a / b            # 3.333... — immer float!
r   = a % b            # 1

import math
root   = math.sqrt(a)          # 3.162...
ceil   = math.ceil(3.2)        # 4
floor  = math.floor(3.8)       # 3
log10  = math.log10(100)       # 2.0
absVal = abs(-5)               # 5
inf    = math.inf
nan    = math.nan
math.isnan(nan)                # True

# divmod — Quotient und Rest in einer Operation
q, r = divmod(a, b)            # (3, 1)

# round (Banker-Rundung in Python!)
round(2.5)                     # 2 — rundet zur nächsten geraden Zahl
round(3.5)                     # 4

# Decimal für genaue Arithmetik (Finanzen etc.)
from decimal import Decimal
Decimal("0.1") + Decimal("0.2") == Decimal("0.3")  # True (im Gegensatz zu float!)

package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "math"
    "math/big"
    "math/bits"
)

func numbers() {
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Ganzzahlen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    var a int   = 100
    var b int64 = 9_223_372_036_854_775_807  // Trennzeichen _ zur besseren Lesbarkeit (Go 1.13+)
    var c uint  = 0xFF                        // Hex-Literal
    var d int   = 0b1010_1010                 // Binär-Literal (Go 1.13+)
    var e int   = 0o755                       // Oktal-Literal (Go 1.13+)
    _ = a; _ = b; _ = c; _ = d; _ = e

    // Ganzzahlarithmetik
    fmt.Println(10 / 3)                       // 3 (abgeschnitten)
    fmt.Println(10 % 3)                       // 1
    fmt.Println(-7 % 3)                       // -1 (Vorzeichen entspricht dem Dividenden)

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Gleitkommazahlen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    x := 3.14
    y := 2.71828
    fmt.Printf("%.4f\n", x + y)              // Formatierung

    // Spezielle Werte
    posInf := math.Inf(1)                     // +∞
    negInf := math.Inf(-1)                    // -∞
    nan    := math.NaN()
    fmt.Println(math.IsInf(posInf, 1))        // true
    fmt.Println(math.IsNaN(nan))              // true

    // Float-Vergleich — NUR über Epsilon!
    const eps = 1e-9
    func floatEqual(a, b float64) bool { return math.Abs(a-b) < eps }

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Mathematische Funktionen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    math.Abs(-5.0)                            // 5.0
    math.Sqrt(16.0)                           // 4.0
    math.Pow(2, 10)                           // 1024.0
    math.Log(math.E)                          // 1.0
    math.Log2(1024)                           // 10.0
    math.Log10(1000)                          // 3.0
    math.Floor(3.9)                           // 3.0
    math.Ceil(3.1)                            // 4.0
    math.Round(3.5)                           // 4.0 (Banker-Rundung: Round(2.5) = 2)
    math.Max(3.0, 5.0)                        // 5.0
    math.Min(3.0, 5.0)                        // 3.0

    // Trigonometrie
    math.Sin(math.Pi / 2)                     // 1.0
    math.Cos(0)                               // 1.0

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Bit-Operationen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    n := 0b1010
    fmt.Println(n & 0b0110)                   // AND: 0b0010 = 2
    fmt.Println(n | 0b0110)                   // OR:  0b1110 = 14
    fmt.Println(n ^ 0b0110)                   // XOR: 0b1100 = 12
    fmt.Println(n << 2)                       // Left Shift: 40
    fmt.Println(n >> 1)                       // Right Shift: 5
    fmt.Println(^n)                           // NOT: -11 (Bitweises Komplement)
    fmt.Println(n &^ 0b0110)                  // AND NOT (Bit löschen): 0b1000 = 8

    // math/bits — Bit-Zählung (Go 1.9+)
    bits.OnesCount(0b1010_1010)               // 4 (gesetzte Bits)
    bits.Len(255)                             // 8 (Bit-Länge)
    bits.LeadingZeros(1)                      // 63 (für uint64)

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // big.Int — beliebige Genauigkeit
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    a_big := new(big.Int).SetInt64(1_000_000_000_000)
    b_big := new(big.Int).SetInt64(999_999_999_999)
    sum   := new(big.Int).Add(a_big, b_big)
    fmt.Println(sum.String())                 // "1999999999999"

    // Fakultät 100
    result := new(big.Int).SetInt64(1)
    for i := int64(2); i <= 100; i++ {
        result.Mul(result, big.NewInt(i))
    }
    fmt.Println(result.String())              // 93326215443944152681699238856266...

    // big.Float — Gleitkomma mit beliebiger Genauigkeit
    pi, _, _ := big.ParseFloat("3.14159265358979323846", 10, 256, big.ToNearestEven)
    fmt.Println(pi.Text('f', 30))             // 30 Nachkommastellen
}

package main

import (
    "fmt"
    "strings"
    "unicode"
    "unicode/utf8"
)

func stringBasics() {
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Literale
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    s  := "Hello, 世界"           // Standardmäßig UTF-8
    raw := `Multiline
raw string \n wird nicht maskiert`// Raw String Literal (Backticks)

    // Länge: Bytes vs Runen!
    fmt.Println(len(s))                       // 13 Bytes (NICHT Zeichen!)
    fmt.Println(utf8.RuneCountInString(s))    // 9 Runen (Zeichen)

    // Iteration — IMMER range für Unicode verwenden
    for i, r := range s {
        fmt.Printf("pos=%d rune=%c (%d)\n", i, r, r)
    }

    // Explizite Konvertierung string ↔ []rune (für Manipulationen)
    runes := []rune(s)
    runes[0] = 'h'
    modified := string(runes)                 // "hello, 世界"

    // string ↔ []byte (für binäre Operationen)
    bytes := []byte(s)
    bytes[0] = 'H'
    _ = string(bytes)                         // "Hello, 世界"
    _ = raw; _ = modified

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Formatierung (fmt.Sprintf)
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    name, age, score := "Alice", 30, 98.5
    fmt.Sprintf("Name: %s, Age: %d, Score: %.2f", name, age, score)
    fmt.Sprintf("Binary: %b, Hex: %x, Oct: %o", 42, 255, 8)
    fmt.Sprintf("Width: %10s, %-10s", "right", "left")   // Ausrichtung
    fmt.Sprintf("Padded: %05d", 42)           // "00042"
    fmt.Sprintf("Pointer: %p", &name)
    fmt.Sprintf("Type: %T, Value: %v", name, name)  // "string", "Alice"
    fmt.Sprintf("Struct: %+v", struct{ X, Y int }{1, 2})  // {X:1 Y:2}

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // strings Package — Basisoperationen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    s2 := "  Hello, World!  "

    strings.TrimSpace(s2)                     // "Hello, World!"
    strings.Trim(s2, " !")                    // "Hello, World"
    strings.TrimLeft(s2, " ")                 // "Hello, World!  "
    strings.TrimRight(s2, " ")                // "  Hello, World!"
    strings.TrimPrefix("photo.jpg", "photo.") // "jpg"
    strings.TrimSuffix("photo.jpg", ".jpg")   // "photo"

    strings.ToUpper("hello")                  // "HELLO"
    strings.ToLower("HELLO")                  // "hello"
    // strings.Title ist veraltet, stattdessen cases.Title verwenden

    strings.HasPrefix("filename.go", "file")  // true
    strings.HasSuffix("filename.go", ".go")   // true
    strings.Contains("hello world", "world")  // true
    strings.ContainsAny("hello", "aeiou")     // true (enthält mindestens einen der Buchstaben)
    strings.Count("cheese", "e")             // 3
    strings.Index("hello", "ll")             // 2 (-1 wenn nicht gefunden)
    strings.LastIndex("hello", "l")          // 3

    strings.Replace("oink oink oink", "oink", "moo", 2) // "moo moo oink"
    strings.ReplaceAll("foo bar foo", "foo", "baz")      // "baz bar baz"

    strings.Split("a,b,c", ",")              // ["a", "b", "c"]
    strings.SplitN("a,b,c", ",", 2)         // ["a", "b,c"]
    strings.Fields("  foo  bar  ")           // ["foo", "bar"] (am Leerzeichen aufteilen)
    strings.Join([]string{"a", "b", "c"}, "-") // "a-b-c"

    strings.Repeat("Go!", 3)                 // "Go!Go!Go!"

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // strings.Builder — effiziente Konkatenation
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    var b strings.Builder
    for i := 0; i < 5; i++ {
        fmt.Fprintf(&b, "item%d ", i)
    }
    result := b.String()                      // "item0 item1 item2 item3 item4 "
    b.Reset()                                 // für erneute Verwendung leeren
    _ = result

    // Operator + Konkatenation (nur für wenige, kurze Strings!)
    greeting := "Hello" + ", " + "World!"    // erstellt 2 Zwischenstrings

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // strconv — Konvertierungen String ↔ Zahlen
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    import "strconv"
    n, err := strconv.Atoi("42")             // string → int
    s3 := strconv.Itoa(42)                   // int → string
    f, _ := strconv.ParseFloat("3.14", 64)   // string → float64
    fs := strconv.FormatFloat(3.14, 'f', 2, 64)  // float64 → string: "3.14"
    b2, _ := strconv.ParseBool("true")       // "true"/"false"/"1"/"0" → bool
    _ = n; _ = err; _ = s3; _ = f; _ = fs; _ = b2
    _ = greeting

    // Unicode Prüfungen
    unicode.IsLetter('A')                     // true
    unicode.IsDigit('5')                      // true
    unicode.IsSpace(' ')                      // true
    unicode.IsUpper('A')                      // true
    unicode.ToLower('A')                      // 'a'
}

// Array (Value Type mit Copy-on-Write)

var filled = [Int](repeating: 0, count: 5)    // [0,0,0,0,0]

arr.append(6)
arr.insert(0, at: 0)                           // 0 an Position 0 einfügen
arr.remove(at: 0)                              // nach Index entfernen
arr.removeLast()
arr.removeFirst()
arr.removeAll(where: { $0 % 2 == 0 })         // alle geraden Zahlen entfernen

arr.count                                      // Anzahl
arr.isEmpty                                    // false
arr.first                                      // Optional(1) — sicher
arr.last                                       // Optional
arr[2]                                         // 3 — Crash bei out of bounds!
arr.indices.contains(2) ? arr[2] : 0          // sicherer Zugriff

arr.contains(3)                                // true
arr.contains(where: { $0 > 3 })
arr.firstIndex(of: 3)                          // Optional(2)

// Sortierung
arr.sort()                                     // in place
let sorted = arr.sorted()                      // neues Array
arr.sort { $0 > $1 }                          // absteigend
let sortedBy = arr.sorted(by: { $0 > $1 })

// Funktionale Operationen
arr.filter { $0 > 2 }                         // [3,4,5]
arr.map { $0 * 2 }                            // [2,4,6,8,10]
arr.reduce(0, +)                               // 15
arr.reduce(into: [:]) { $0[$1] = $1 * 2 }    // Dictionary erzeugen
arr.compactMap { Int("\($0)") }               // map + nil entfernen

// Slices (ArraySlice — kopiert die Daten nicht!)

let slice2 = arr.prefix(3)                     // erste 3

// Zip
let pairs = zip([1,2,3], ["a","b","c"])        // [(1,"a"),(2,"b"),(3,"c")]

// WICHTIG: immutable / mutable unterscheiden!
val immutable: List<Int> = listOf(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)    // read-only View
val mutable: MutableList<Int> = mutableListOf(1, 2, 3)

val list = arrayListOf(1, 2, 3, 4, 5)               // MutableList

list.add(6)
list.addAll(listOf(7, 8, 9))
list.add(0, 0)                                       // 0 an Position 0 einfügen

list.size
list.isEmpty()
list.first()                                         // wirft NoSuchElementException!
list.firstOrNull()                                   // null wenn leer — sicher
list.last(); list.lastOrNull()
list.getOrNull(10)                                   // null bei out of bounds

// Sortierung

val sorted = list.sorted()                           // neue Liste
list.sortedBy { it }
list.sortedByDescending { it }
list.sortedWith(compareBy({ it.length }, { it }))   // mehrstufig

// Funktionale Operationen
list.filter { it > 2 }
list.map { it * 2 }
list.reduce { acc, i -> acc + i }
list.fold(0) { acc, i -> acc + i }                  // wie reduce, aber mit Initialwert
list.sumOf { it }
list.maxOrNull(); list.minOrNull()
list.flatMap { listOf(it, it * 10) }
list.mapNotNull { if (it > 3) it * 2 else null }    // map + null filtern

// Gruppierung
list.groupBy { it % 2 == 0 }                        // Map<Boolean, List<Int>>
list.partition { it > 3 }                           // Pair<List, List>
list.windowed(3)                                    // Sliding Window

// Destrukturierung
val (first, second, third) = listOf(1, 2, 3)

// Zip
val pairs = listOf(1,2,3).zip(listOf("a","b","c"))  // [(1,a),(2,b),(3,c)]

# list (dynamisches Array, alle Elemente sind Referenzen auf Objekte)
arr: list[int] = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]

arr.append(6)                                  # ans Ende anhängen — O(1)
arr.insert(0, 0)                               # 0 an Position 0 einfügen — O(n)!
arr.pop()                                      # letztes Element entfernen und zurückgeben — O(1)
arr.pop(0)                                     # nach Index entfernen — O(n)!
arr.remove(3)                                  # erstes Element == 3 entfernen
arr.extend([7, 8, 9])                          # mehrere anhängen

len(arr)
arr[0]; arr[-1]                                # erstes / letztes
arr[10] if 10 < len(arr) else 0               # sicherer Zugriff

3 in arr                                       # True
arr.index(3)                                   # Index des ersten Treffers
arr.count(3)                                   # wie oft es vorkommt

# Sortierung
arr.sort()                                     # in place (schneller)
sorted_arr = sorted(arr)                       # neue Liste
arr.sort(reverse=True)                         # absteigend
arr.sort(key=lambda x: -x)                    # mit Schlüssel
sorted(words, key=str.lower)                  # ohne Beachtung der Groß-/Kleinschreibung

# Funktionale Operationen

# map/filter/reduce (weniger pythonic, funktionieren aber)

mapped   = list(map(lambda x: x * 2, arr))
from functools import reduce

# Slices — extrem mächtiges Werkzeug
arr[1:4]                                       # [2,3,4]
arr[:3]                                        # erste 3
arr[-2:]                                       # letzte 2

# Gruppierung

grouped = {k: list(v) for k, v in groupby(sorted(arr), key=lambda x: x % 2)}

# zip — parallele Iteration

    print(a, b)

# enumerate — Index + Wert

    print(f"{i}: {v}")

package main

import (
    "cmp"
    "maps"
    "slices"      // Go 1.21+
    "fmt"
)

func collections() {
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Array — feste Größe, Value Type (wird kopiert)
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    var arr int                            // [0 0 0 0 0][4]
    arr2 := string{"a", "b", "c"}[5]
    arr3 := [...]int{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}          // Compiler ermittelt die Größe
    fmt.Println(len(arr3))                    // 5
    _ = arr; _ = arr2

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Slice — dynamisches Array (Referenztyp)
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    s := []int{1, 2, 3, 4, 5}
    s2 := make([]int, 5)                      // len=5, cap=5, mit Nullen gefüllt
    s3 := make([]int, 0, 10)                  // len=0, cap=10 (Vorab-Allokation)

    // Slices
    s[1:3]                                    // [2 3] (len=2)
    s[:3]                                     // [1 2 3]
    s[2:]                                     // [3 4 5]
    s[:]                                      // Header-Kopie, dieselben Daten!
    s[1:3:4]                                  // full slice expression (cap=3)

    // append — kann reallokieren!
    s2 = append(s2, 6)
    s2 = append(s2, 7, 8, 9)                 // mehrere Elemente
    s2 = append(s2, s3...)                   // anderes Slice spreaden

    // copy — sicheres Kopieren
    dst := make([]int, len(s))
    n := copy(dst, s)                         // kopiert min(len(dst), len(s)) Elemente
    _ = n

    // Go-1.21+-Paket slices (ersetzt manuelle Implementierungen)
    slices.Contains(s, 3)                     // true
    slices.Index(s, 3)                        // 2
    slices.Max(s)                             // 5
    slices.Min(s)                             // 1
    slices.Reverse(s)                         // in-place umdrehen
    slices.Sort(s)                            // in-place sortieren
    slices.SortFunc(s, func(a, b int) int { return cmp.Compare(a, b) })
    slices.BinarySearch(s, 3)                 // (2, true)
    slices.Equal(s, dst)                      // elementweiser Vergleich
    slices.Clone(s)                           // shallow copy
    slices.Compact([]int{1,1,2,3,3})          // [1 2 3] (benachbarte Duplikate entfernen)
    slices.Delete(s, 1, 3)                    // Elemente [1:3) entfernen
    slices.Insert(s, 2, 99, 100)              // an Position 2 einfügen
    slices.Collect(slices.Values(s))          // iter → slice (Go 1.23+)
    _ = s2; _ = s3

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Map — Hash-Tabelle (Swiss Tables seit Go 1.24!)
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    m := map[string]int{
        "apple":  5,
        "banana": 3,
        "cherry": 12,
    }
    m2 := make(map[string]int)                // leere Map
    m2["key"] = 42

    // Zugriff — IMMER den zweiten Wert prüfen
    v, ok := m["apple"]                       // v=5, ok=true
    v2, ok2 := m["mango"]                     // v2=0, ok2=false (Zero Value!)
    _ = v; _ = ok; _ = v2; _ = ok2

    // Löschen
    delete(m, "banana")

    // Iteration (Reihenfolge ist NICHT garantiert!)
    for k, v3 := range m {
        fmt.Printf("%s: %d\n", k, v3)
    }

    // Existenzprüfung
    if count, exists := m["cherry"]; exists {
        fmt.Println(count)
    }

    // Go-1.21+-Paket maps
    maps.Clone(m)                             // shallow copy
    maps.Keys(m)                              // Schlüssel-Iterator (Go 1.23)
    maps.Values(m)                            // Wert-Iterator
    maps.Equal(m, m2)                         // Vergleich
    maps.Delete(m, func(k string, v int) bool { return v < 5 })  // bedingtes Löschen
    maps.Collect(maps.All(m))                 // iter → map

    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    // Set — kein eingebauter Typ, wird über map emuliert
    // ──────────────────────────────────────────
    type Set[T comparable] map[T]struct{}

    // Erstellen
    set := Set[string]{"a": {}, "b": {}, "c": {}}

    // Hinzufügen
    set["d"] = struct{}{}

    // Enthält
    _, has := set["a"]                        // true

    // Entfernen
    delete(set, "b")

    // Iteration
    for item := range set {
        _ = item
    }
    _ = has

    // Oder über slices → deduplizieren
    unique := slices.Compact(slices.Sorted(slices.Values([]int{1,2,2,3,3,4})))
    fmt.Println(unique)                       // [1 2 3 4]
}

# Erstellen
specific = datetime(2026, 3, 15, 10, 30, 0, tzinfo=ZoneInfo("Europe/Berlin"))

# Komponenten
year  = now.year
month = now.month
day   = now.day
dow   = now.weekday()                          # 0=Mo, 6=So
dow2  = now.isoweekday()                       # 1=Mo, 7=So

# Formatierung

# Parsing

# Arithmetik
tomorrow  = today + timedelta(days=1)
next_week = today + timedelta(weeks=1)

# relativedelta — Monate und Jahre (timedelta kann das nicht)
next_month = today + relativedelta(months=1)
next_year  = today + relativedelta(years=1)
age = relativedelta(today, date(1990, 1, 1))
print(f"{age.years} Jahre, {age.months} Monate")

# Timestamp (Unix-Epoche)
ts = now.timestamp()                           # float
from_ts = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts, tz=timezone.utc)

# Performance-Messung
import time
start = time.perf_counter()
heavy_work()
elapsed = time.perf_counter() - start
print(f"{elapsed:.4f}s")

# monotonic (unabhängig von der Systemuhr)
mono_start = time.monotonic()

// QR-Code-Generierung über Core Image
func generateQR(from string: String, size: CGSize = CGSize(width: 200, height: 200)) -> UIImage? {
    guard let data = string.data(using: .ascii),
          let filter = CIFilter(name: "CIQRCodeGenerator")
    else { return nil }
    filter.setValue(data, forKey: "inputMessage")
    filter.setValue("H", forKey: "inputCorrectionLevel")
    guard let output = filter.outputImage else { return nil }

    let scaleX = size.width  / output.extent.size.width
    let scaleY = size.height / output.extent.size.height
    let scaled = output.transformed(by: CGAffineTransform(scaleX: scaleX, y: scaleY))
    guard let cgOut = CIContext().createCGImage(scaled, from: scaled.extent)
    else { return nil }
    return UIImage(cgImage: cgOut)
}

// Speichern
fun Bitmap.saveAsJpeg(path: String, quality: Int = 85) {
    java.io.FileOutputStream(path).use { fos ->
        compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.JPEG, quality, fos)
    }
}

fun Bitmap.saveAsPng(path: String) {
    java.io.FileOutputStream(path).use { fos ->
        compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.PNG, 100, fos)
    }
}

fun Bitmap.toBase64Jpeg(quality: Int = 80): String {
    val stream = java.io.ByteArrayOutputStream()
    compress(Bitmap.CompressFormat.JPEG, quality, stream)
    return android.util.Base64.encodeToString(
        stream.toByteArray(), android.util.Base64.DEFAULT
    )
}

# QR mit zentriertem Logo
def qr_with_logo(data: str, logo_path: str, output: str) -> None:
    qr = qrcode.QRCode(error_correction=qrcode.constants.ERROR_CORRECT_H)
    qr.add_data(data)
    qr.make(fit=True)
    qr_img = qr.make_image(fill_color="black", back_color="white").convert("RGBA")

    logo = Image.open(logo_path).convert("RGBA")
    logo_size = qr_img.size[0] // 4
    logo = logo.resize((logo_size, logo_size), Image.LANCZOS)

    pos = ((qr_img.size[0] - logo_size) // 2, (qr_img.size[1] - logo_size) // 2)
    qr_img.paste(logo, pos, mask=logo)
    qr_img.save(output)

Nur Kotlin

KMP ist eine einzigartige Kotlin-Funktion für die plattformübergreifende Entwicklung: eine Codebasis für iOS, Android, Desktop und Web.

// ── Projektstruktur ──
// my-project/
// ├── shared/                    ← gemeinsames KMP-Modul
// │   ├── src/
// │   │   ├── commonMain/        ← gemeinsamer Code (alle Plattformen)
// │   │   ├── commonTest/
// │   │   ├── androidMain/       ← Android-spezifisch
// │   │   ├── iosMain/           ← iOS-spezifisch
// │   │   └── desktopMain/       ← Desktop-spezifisch
// ├── androidApp/                ← Android-Anwendung
// ├── iosApp/                    ← iOS-Anwendung (Xcode)
// └── desktopApp/                ← Desktop-Anwendung

// shared/build.gradle.kts
kotlin {
    androidTarget { compilations.all { kotlinOptions { jvmTarget = "17" } } }
    iosX64(); iosArm64(); iosSimulatorArm64()
    jvm("desktop")

    sourceSets {
        commonMain.dependencies {
            implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-coroutines-core:1.9.0")
            implementation("org.jetbrains.kotlinx:kotlinx-serialization-json:1.7.3")
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-core:3.0.0")
        }
        androidMain.dependencies {
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-android:3.0.0")
        }
        iosMain.dependencies {
            implementation("io.ktor:ktor-client-darwin:3.0.0")
        }
    }
}

// ── expect / actual — plattformabhängiger Code ──

// commonMain: den „Vertrag“ deklarieren
expect class PlatformInfo() {
    val name: String
    val version: String
}

expect fun currentTimeMillis(): Long

// androidMain: Implementierung für Android
actual class PlatformInfo actual constructor() {
    actual val name = "Android"
    actual val version = android.os.Build.VERSION.RELEASE
}

actual fun currentTimeMillis(): Long = System.currentTimeMillis()

// iosMain: Implementierung für iOS
actual class PlatformInfo actual constructor() {
    actual val name = "iOS"
    actual val version = UIDevice.currentDevice.systemVersion
}

actual fun currentTimeMillis(): Long =
    (NSDate().timeIntervalSince1970 * 1000).toLong()

// ── Gemeinsamer Code (commonMain) ──

interface UserRepository {
    suspend fun getUsers(): List<User>
    suspend fun getUserById(id: Int): User?
}

class UserViewModel(private val repo: UserRepository) {
    private val _state = MutableStateFlow<UserState>(UserState.Loading)
    val state: StateFlow<UserState> = _state.asStateFlow()

    fun loadUsers() {
        viewModelScope.launch {
            _state.value = try {
                UserState.Success(repo.getUsers())
            } catch (e: Exception) {
                UserState.Error(e.message ?: "Unknown error")
            }
        }
    }
}

sealed class UserState {
    data object Loading : UserState()
    data class Success(val users: List<User>) : UserState()
    data class Error(val message: String) : UserState()
}

AES-GCM

Authenticated Encryption (AEAD): verschlüsselt Daten und prüft gleichzeitig ihre Integrität. Empfohlen für alle neuen Projekte.

Swift 6Kotlin 2.xPython 3.13Go 1.24+ 
import CryptoKit

func encrypt(_ plaintext: Data, with key: SymmetricKey) throws -> Data {
    let sealedBox = try AES.GCM.seal(plaintext, using: key)
    return sealedBox.combined!                 // nonce + ciphertext + tag
}

func decrypt(_ cipherData: Data, with key: SymmetricKey) throws -> Data {
    let sealedBox = try AES.GCM.SealedBox(combined: cipherData)
    return try AES.GCM.open(sealedBox, using: key)
}

let symmetricKey = SymmetricKey(size: .bits256)
let secret = "Secret message".data(using: .utf8)!
let encrypted = try encrypt(secret, with: symmetricKey)
let base64     = encrypted.base64EncodedString()

let decrypted = try decrypt(Data(base64Encoded: base64)!, with: symmetricKey)
let original  = String(data: decrypted, encoding: .utf8)  // "Secret message"

// ChaCha20-Poly1305 — schneller auf mobilen Geräten ohne AES-Hardwarebeschleunigung
let chaBox = try ChaChaPoly.seal(secret, using: symmetricKey)
let decoded = try ChaChaPoly.open(chaBox, using: symmetricKey)

import javax.crypto.*
import javax.crypto.spec.GCMParameterSpec

object AesGcm {
    private const val ALGORITHM = "AES/GCM/NoPadding"
    private const val KEY_SIZE  = 256
    private const val NONCE_LEN = 12           // 96 bit — empfohlen
    private const val TAG_LEN   = 128          // bits

    fun generateKey(): SecretKey =
        KeyGenerator.getInstance("AES").apply { init(KEY_SIZE) }.generateKey()

    fun encrypt(plaintext: ByteArray, key: SecretKey): ByteArray {
        val nonce  = ByteArray(NONCE_LEN).also {
            java.security.SecureRandom().nextBytes(it)
        }
        val cipher = Cipher.getInstance(ALGORITHM).apply {
            init(Cipher.ENCRYPT_MODE, key, GCMParameterSpec(TAG_LEN, nonce))
        }
        val ciphertext = cipher.doFinal(plaintext)
        return nonce + ciphertext              // nonce || ciphertext+tag
    }

    fun decrypt(combined: ByteArray, key: SecretKey): ByteArray {
        val nonce      = combined.copyOfRange(0, NONCE_LEN)
        val ciphertext = combined.copyOfRange(NONCE_LEN, combined.size)
        return Cipher.getInstance(ALGORITHM).run {
            init(Cipher.DECRYPT_MODE, key, GCMParameterSpec(TAG_LEN, nonce))
            doFinal(ciphertext)
        }
    }
}

val key       = AesGcm.generateKey()
val encrypted = AesGcm.encrypt("Secret message".toByteArray(), key)
val decrypted = AesGcm.decrypt(encrypted, key)
println(String(decrypted))                     // "Secret message"

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.ciphers.aead import AESGCM
import secrets, base64

def encrypt(plaintext: bytes, key: bytes | None = None) -> tuple[bytes, bytes]:
    """Gibt (key, nonce+ciphertext) zurück"""
    if key is None:
        key = AESGCM.generate_key(bit_length=256)
    aesgcm = AESGCM(key)
    nonce  = secrets.token_bytes(12)           # 96 bit
    ct     = aesgcm.encrypt(nonce, plaintext, associated_data=None)
    return key, nonce + ct

def decrypt(combined: bytes, key: bytes) -> bytes:
    nonce, ct = combined[:12], combined[12:]
    return AESGCM(key).decrypt(nonce, ct, associated_data=None)

key, enc = encrypt(b"Secret message")
b64_enc  = base64.b64encode(enc).decode()
dec = decrypt(base64.b64decode(b64_enc), key)
print(dec.decode())                            // "Secret message"

// ③ AES-GCM — symmetrische Verschlüsselung
// GCM = Galois/Counter Mode: authenticated encryption (Verschlüsselung + MAC)
// WICHTIG: Nonce niemals mit demselben Schlüssel wiederverwenden!
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func encrypt(plaintext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
    if len(key) != 16 && len(key) != 24 && len(key) != 32 {
        return nil, errors.New("key must be 16, 24, or 32 bytes (AES-128/192/256)")
    }

    block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    // Nonce: 12 Byte, kryptografisch zufällig
    nonce := make([]byte, gcm.NonceSize())    // NonceSize() = 12
    if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, nonce); err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("generate nonce: %w", err)
    }

    // Seal: verschlüsselt UND fügt das Authentication-Tag an (16 Byte am Ende)
    // Format: [nonce(12) || ciphertext || tag(16)]
    ciphertext := gcm.Seal(nonce, nonce, plaintext, nil)
    return ciphertext, nil
}

func decrypt(ciphertext, key []byte) ([]byte, error) {
    block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    gcm, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    nonceSize := gcm.NonceSize()
    if len(ciphertext) < nonceSize {
        return nil, errors.New("ciphertext too short")
    }

    nonce, ciphertext := ciphertext[:nonceSize], ciphertext[nonceSize:]

    // Open: entschlüsselt UND prüft das Authentication-Tag
    plaintext, err := gcm.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, nil)
    if err != nil {
        // Keine Details preisgeben — nur "decryption failed"
        return nil, errors.New("decryption failed")
    }
    return plaintext, nil
}

// Schlüssel aus Passwort über PBKDF2/Argon2 ableiten
func deriveKey(password, salt []byte) []byte {
    // Argon2id → 32-Byte-Schlüssel für AES-256
    return argon2.IDKey(password, salt, 3, 64*1024, 4, 32)
}

func encryptWithPassword(plaintext []byte, password string) ([]byte, error) {
    salt := make([]byte, 16)
    if _, err := io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, salt); err != nil { return nil, err }

    key := deriveKey([]byte(password), salt)
    encrypted, err := encrypt(plaintext, key)
    if err != nil { return nil, err }

    // Format: [salt(16) || encrypted_data]
    result := make([]byte, len(salt)+len(encrypted))
    copy(result, salt)
    copy(result[len(salt):], encrypted)
    return result, nil
}

func decryptWithPassword(data []byte, password string) ([]byte, error) {
    if len(data) < 16 { return nil, errors.New("data too short") }
    salt, encrypted := data[:16], data[16:]
    key := deriveKey([]byte(password), salt)
    return decrypt(encrypted, key)
}

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ④ JWT — github.com/golang-jwt/jwt/v5
// Für Produktion wird ES256 (asymmetrisch) empfohlen
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
type Claims struct {
    UserID string `json:"uid"`
    Email  string `json:"email"`
    Role   string `json:"role"`
    jwt.RegisteredClaims
}

type JWTManager struct {
    signingKey     []byte         // für HS256
    accessExpiry   time.Duration
    refreshExpiry  time.Duration
    issuer         string
}

func NewJWTManager(secret string) *JWTManager {
    return &JWTManager{
        signingKey:    []byte(secret),
        accessExpiry:  15 * time.Minute,  // kurze Laufzeit!
        refreshExpiry: 7 * 24 * time.Hour,
        issuer:        "myapp",
    }
}

func (m *JWTManager) GenerateAccessToken(userID, email, role string) (string, error) {
    claims := Claims{
        UserID: userID,
        Email:  email,
        Role:   role,
        RegisteredClaims: jwt.RegisteredClaims{
            Issuer:    m.issuer,
            Subject:   userID,
            ExpiresAt: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now().Add(m.accessExpiry)),
            IssuedAt:  jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
            NotBefore: jwt.NewNumericDate(time.Now()),
            ID:        generateTokenID(),           // jti: eindeutige ID für Widerruf
        },
    }
    token := jwt.NewWithClaims(jwt.SigningMethodHS256, claims)
    return token.SignedString(m.signingKey)
}

func (m *JWTManager) Verify(tokenString string) (*Claims, error) {
    token, err := jwt.ParseWithClaims(
        tokenString,
        &Claims{},
        func(t *jwt.Token) (any, error) {
            // Algorithmus MUSS geprüft werden — Schutz vor alg=none-Angriff
            if _, ok := t.Method.(*jwt.SigningMethodHMAC); !ok {
                return nil, fmt.Errorf("unexpected signing method: %v", t.Header["alg"])
            }
            return m.signingKey, nil
        },
        jwt.WithIssuer(m.issuer),
        jwt.WithExpirationRequired(),          // exp-Claim erforderlich
        jwt.WithValidMethods([]string{"HS256"}), // Whitelist der Algorithmen
    )

    if err != nil {
        switch {
        case errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrTokenExpired):
            return nil, ErrTokenExpired
        case errors.Is(err, jwt.ErrTokenSignatureInvalid):
            return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
        default:
            return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse token: %w", err)
        }
    }

    claims, ok := token.Claims.(*Claims)
    if !ok || !token.Valid {
        return nil, ErrTokenInvalid
    }
    return claims, nil
}

// JWT-Middleware für net/http
func (m *JWTManager) Middleware(next http.Handler) http.Handler {
    return http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
        authHeader := r.Header.Get("Authorization")
        if authHeader == "" || !strings.HasPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ") {
            http.Error(w, `{"error":"missing authorization header"}`,
                http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }

        tokenString := strings.TrimPrefix(authHeader, "Bearer ")
        claims, err := m.Verify(tokenString)
        if err != nil {
            http.Error(w, fmt.Sprintf(`{"error":%q}`, err.Error()),
                http.StatusUnauthorized)
            return
        }

        // Claims zum Context hinzufügen
        ctx := context.WithValue(r.Context(), claimsKey, claims)
        next.ServeHTTP(w, r.WithContext(ctx))
    })
}

// Sentinel-Fehler für JWT
var (
    ErrTokenExpired = errors.New("token expired")
    ErrTokenInvalid = errors.New("token invalid")
)

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ⑤ Sichere Generierung von Zufallsdaten
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func secureRandom() {
    // Session-Token
    token := make([]byte, 32)
    io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, token)
    sessionToken := base64.URLEncoding.EncodeToString(token) // URL-safe

    // Zufallszeichenkette (für CSRF-Token, API-Keys)
    apiKey := make([]byte, 24)
    io.ReadFull(rand.Reader, apiKey)
    key := base64.RawURLEncoding.EncodeToString(apiKey)

    // Zufallszahl im Bereich [0, max)
    import "math/big"
    max := big.NewInt(1_000_000)
    n, _ := rand.Int(rand.Reader, max)        // kryptografisch sicher!
    otp := n.Int64()                          // OTP-Code

    _ = sessionToken; _ = key; _ = otp
}

// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
// ⑥ constant-time Vergleich — gegen Timing-Angriffe
// ──────────────────────────────────────────────
func safeCompare(a, b string) bool {
    // subtle.ConstantTimeCompare — gleiche Zeit unabhängig von Gleichheit
    return subtle.ConstantTimeCompare([]byte(a), []byte(b)) == 1
}

func validateAPIKey(provided, expected string) bool {
    // NIEMALS == zum Vergleichen von Geheimnissen verwenden!
    // if provided == expected { ... }        // VERWUNDBAR für Timing-Angriffe
    return safeCompare(provided, expected)    // SICHER
}

Nur Swift

Makros sind eine einzigartige Swift-Funktion. Kotlin verwendet KSP/KotlinPoet für Codegenerierung, Python verwendet Dekoratoren und Metaklassen.

// ── Eingebaute / Apple-Makros ──

// @Observable (Swift 5.9+, ersetzt ObservableObject + @Published)
import Observation

@Observable
class ViewModel {
    var items: [Item] = []
    var isLoading: Bool = false
    var errorMessage: String? = nil

    func load() async {
        isLoading = true
        defer { isLoading = false }
        items = try! await fetchItems()
    }
}

// In SwiftUI — @StateObject / @ObservedObject werden nicht benötigt
struct ContentView: View {
    @State private var vm = ViewModel()

    var body: some View {
        if vm.isLoading { ProgressView() }
        else { List(vm.items) { Text($0.name) } }
    }
}

// #Preview (Swift 5.9+)
#Preview {
    ContentView()
}

// #stringify — eingebautes Expression-Makro
let (value, string) = #stringify(2 + 3)
// value = 5, string = "2 + 3"

// ── Eigenes Makro erstellen ──
// Erfordert ein separates Package-Target vom Typ .macro

import SwiftSyntax
import SwiftSyntaxMacros

public struct UnwrappedMacro: ExpressionMacro {
    public static func expansion(
        of node: some FreestandingMacroExpansionSyntax,
        in context: some MacroExpansionContext
    ) throws -> ExprSyntax {
        let arg = node.argumentList.first!.expression
        return "\(arg) ?? { fatalError(\"Unexpected nil: \(arg)\") }()"
    }
}

// Makro-Deklaration in der Public API
@freestanding(expression)
public macro Unwrapped<T>(_ value: T?) -> T = #externalMacro(
    module: "MyMacrosImpl",
    type: "UnwrappedMacro"
)

// Verwendung
let value = #Unwrapped(optionalValue)

// ── Attached Macros ──

@attached(extension, conformances: Equatable, names: named(==))
public macro AutoEquatable() = #externalMacro(
    module: "MyMacrosImpl", type: "AutoEquatableMacro"
)

@AutoEquatable
struct Point {
    var x: Double
    var y: Double
    var label: String
    // Generierter Code:
    // static func == (lhs: Point, rhs: Point) -> Bool {
    //     lhs.x == rhs.x && lhs.y == rhs.y && lhs.label == rhs.label
    // }
}

// Makro-Typen:
// @freestanding(expression) — #macro() in Ausdrücken
// @freestanding(declaration) — #macro() für Deklarationen
// @attached(member)          — fügt einem Typ Member hinzu
// @attached(memberAttribute) — fügt Membern Attribute hinzu
// @attached(accessor)        — fügt get/set hinzu
// @attached(extension)       — fügt eine extension mit Conformance hinzu
// @attached(peer)            — generiert daneben neue Deklarationen
